title: AI-First MERN Stack Developer
job_description:
Troopr Labs  |  Remote (India)  |  1+ Years  |  Full-Time
Product: Enjo.ai  —  Enterprise AI for service & support automation


About Troopr Labs
We build AI products for modern enterprises. ~30-person team. Bootstrapped and profitable. Our products are used by 100,000+ users at companies like Starbucks, Netflix, Snowflake, and Spotify.
Our engineering team is building bleeding-edge agentic AI products for engineering, service, and ops teams — including AI agents, RAG systems, multi-agent orchestration, and enterprise-grade AI workflows.


The Role
This is a high-ownership engineering role. You will build product features end-to-end on the MERN stack while aggressively leveraging AI across the entire development lifecycle — coding, debugging, architecture, refactoring, testing, and documentation.
We don’t want someone who occasionally uses Copilot. We want someone who can’t imagine shipping code without AI. You should already be using tools like Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, v0, Bolt, or similar — and have strong opinions about which ones work best for what.


What You’ll Do
1. Build and own end-to-end product features: APIs, UI, database, deployment.
2. Develop scalable Node.js + Express APIs and modern React applications with clean component architecture.
3. Design and optimize MongoDB schemas and queries for performance at scale.
4. Use AI-powered tools for rapid prototyping, code generation, debugging, and refactoring — daily, not occasionally.
5. Ship fast. We value working software over perfect plans. You’ll prototype, validate, and iterate quickly with engineering and product teams.


What We’re Looking For

Must-Have
1. 1+ years building full-stack applications with MongoDB, Express.js, React, and Node.js.
2. Demonstrated AI-first workflow — show us your setup. We’ll ask you to walk through how you use AI tools in your actual dev process.
3. Strong fundamentals: data structures, API design, async patterns, component architecture.
4. Clear written and verbal communication. We’re remote-first; async clarity matters.

Strong Signals
1. You’ve built something non-trivial with AI tools (side project, open-source contribution, automation).
2. Prior experience in a B2B SaaS product environment.
3. Familiarity with LLM APIs, prompt engineering, or building AI-powered features.
4. You stay curious — you follow AI tooling releases, experiment with new dev workflows, and form opinions.


Why This Role
1. You’ll work on real agentic AI products used by large enterprises — not demos or proof-of-concepts.
2. Small team, direct ownership, fast shipping cycles. No bureaucracy.
3. Exposure to the full stack of enterprise AI: RAG, agents, orchestration, multi-LLM systems.
4. Bootstrapped and profitable — we build with intent, not burn.
5. Remote-first culture. Work from anywhere in India.


How to Apply
Send us your resume and a short note answering one question:
“Describe your AI-first dev setup — what tools do you use, how has it changed how you write code, and what’s one thing you built faster because of it?”

Applications without this note will not be prioritized.

Apply: careers@troopr.ai  |  enjo.ai/careers

You will use and help set up agentic AI systems to automate parts of the content workflow - research, analysis, briefs, drafting support, optimization, and publishing ops - while ensuring clear human judgment, high-quality inputs, and strong editorial feedback loops throughout.

What You’ll Do
1. Understand the product, audience, and positioning quickly
2. Analyze competitor content strategies and identify content gaps
3. Conduct keyword research and build an initial SEO content backlog
4. Write and publish 4–6 SEO-focused blog posts in the first 30 days, then consistently publish 2–3 high-quality posts per week
5. Create content targeting high-intent search queries with strong on-page SEO fundamentals
6. Set up and manage AI-assisted / agentic content workflows with clear human review and quality control
7. Establish a repeatable research, writing, and optimization process
8. Monitor performance, improve existing content based on insights, and expand the content pipeline over time

What We’re Looking For
1. 2+ years of experience in SEO, content marketing, or copywriting
2. Strong understanding of how SEO content works and how blogs rank on search engines
3. Experience with keyword research, competitor analysis, and content optimization
4. Ability to consistently deliver high-quality content at speed without compromising quality
5. Strong familiarity with AI tools and a practical mindset for building AI-first workflows with human oversight
6. Hands-on experience with tools such as Ahrefs, SEMrush, Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Surfer SEO/Clearscope, and AI tools like ChatGPT or similar


Why This Role Matters
You will help build the organic acquisition engine for a fast-moving AI SaaS company, with direct ownership of outcomes and room to grow quickly.

How to Apply
Send us examples of your work and links to published articles or your portfolio with the hiring team. Candidates whose content ranks on Google or appears in LLM-generated responses will be strongly preferred.

title: Demand Generation Lead – B2B SaaS
job_description:
Experience Required: 3+ years (B2B SaaS demand gen / outbound / quota-carrying)
Location: Remote (India; Bangalore preferred)
Company: Troopr Labs
Website: www.enjo.ai

About Troopr Labs & Enjo

Troopr Labs builds Enjo, a world-class support automation product trusted by hundreds of US enterprises, including Starbucks, Netflix, Snowflake, Spotify, Wayfair, Aurora, and Snap. We are a small, high-ownership team building AI-native workflows that connect into systems like Zendesk, Intercom, and Salesforce to speed up resolution and improve support outcomes.

Role Description

This is a revenue-linked GTM role: you’ll own qualified pipeline generation for US SMB + Mid-Market, combining hands-on execution with systems thinking. You’ll build repeatable outbound + inbound motions, run structured experiments weekly, and instrument the funnel end-to-end.

A Glimpse into Your Day-to-Day

1. Build and run a scalable outbound engine (email + LinkedIn), including tooling and automation (e.g., Clay).
2. Own outbound infrastructure: domains, inbox rotation, warm-up, sequencing, throttling, deliverability monitoring.
3. Launch and iterate paid inbound experiments (Google Search, LinkedIn Ads, retargeting) optimized for qualified meetings.
4. Define ICP segmentation and evolve messaging based on conversion data + sales/customer feedback.
5. Instrument and report funnel performance (lead → meeting → opp → closed-won); keep CRM clean and actionable.

Who We’re Looking For

1. 3+ years in B2B SaaS demand gen/outbound/sales, with clear examples of pipeline you personally drove.
2. Strong grasp of outbound fundamentals (especially deliverability) and modern GTM tooling.
3. Comfortable running experiments: hypotheses, execution, measurement, iteration.
4. Writes clearly, can translate ICP insights into messaging that converts.
5. AI-native operator with high ownership and a bias toward shipping.

Why Join Troopr Labs?

1. Small team, real leverage: your work directly impacts growth for a product used by global enterprises.
2. Build the GTM engine: meaningful ownership across channels, systems, and experimentation.
3. AI at the core: use and build with agentic AI workflows to move faster and smarter.
4. Remote-first, async-friendly: autonomy, focus time, and direct collaboration with founders.

If you want to build a repeatable GTM motion for an AI product already operating at enterprise scale, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now.

title: Design Engineer
job_description:
**The Opportunity**
At Certa, we are building the AI-native Operating System for global enterprise relationships. We have the clients (Fortune 100/500) and the robust backend technology. Now, under the guidance of our new Head of Design, we are elevating our experience to match the best consumer products in the world.
We are looking for our First Design Engineer. You are the "missing link" between our pragmatic engineering requirements and our ambitious design vision. You won't just implement tickets; you will define how our product feels.

**The Role**
This is not a standard Frontend Engineer role, nor is it a Product Designer role. This role is equal parts:
Craft-focused Frontend Engineering: You write production-grade code, but you obsess over the details that others miss.
Systems Thinking: You don't just build components; you build the architecture (Design System) that allows the rest of the team to build beautiful UI faster.
Figma-to-Code Mastery: You bridge the gap between static Figma files and dynamic browser realities, moving fluidly between design and implementation.

**What You Will Do**
1. Architect the Design System & UI Patterns
Take ownership of our internal design system. You will treat it as an internal product, creating a type-safe, accessible, and composable library in React & TypeScript.
Manage the tokens, components, patterns, documentation, and governance required to scale design across a remote engineering team.
Bridge the gap between Figma Variables/Tokens and code, ensuring a single source of truth for our visual language.
2. Make AI-Native UX Feel Trustworthy & Human
Implement interaction patterns specifically for AI workflows: streaming states, progressive disclosure, and optimistic UI.
Build for "explainability" and human-in-the-loop controls, ensuring users trust the system even when it automates complex tasks.
Make complexity feel simple—especially when the system is performing sophisticated "smart" operations in the background.
3. Maximize Output with AI-Assisted Tooling
Hyper-automate the mundane: Use AI coding assistants (Cursor, Copilot, etc.) to handle boilerplate and scaffolding, allowing you to spend 80% of your time on the 20% of the "feel" and "craft."
Build tooling and guardrails (linting rules, visual checks, component playground workflows) that help the team ship high-fidelity UI consistently.
4. Bridge Design & Code (Figma-First)
Live in Figma: You are expected to be as comfortable in Figma as you are in VS Code. You’ll tweak layouts, design missing states, and refine prototypes yourself.
Push performance beyond just metrics: focus on rendering, bundle size, perceived speed, and interaction responsiveness.
Raise the bar on accessibility and cross-browser behavior, ensuring the product is usable by everyone.

**Must Haves**
1.  Expert Figma Skills: This is a primary requirement. You should be fluent in auto-layout, components, variables, and prototyping. You view Figma as a design environment and a technical spec.
2.  AI-Assisted Workflow: You are a "power user" of AI tools (Cursor, Copilot, v0, etc.). You know how to leverage AI to move faster without sacrificing the quality of the final output.
3.  Strong Frontend Foundation: Deep expertise in React + TypeScript with a track record of shipping complex, data-heavy UIs in production.
4.  Demonstrated UI Craft: A portfolio showing obsession with spacing, typography, interaction detail, and motion. You likely have a "Folder of Inspiration" from the best apps in the world.
5.  Design System Experience: Strong experience building and maintaining a design system: tokens, components, patterns, and documentation.
6.  Accessibility Expertise: You build keyboard-first UX, understand screen reader behavior, and enforce correct ARIA usage by default.
7.  Modern Tooling: Experience with Vite and modern frontend tooling; comfortable setting up and customizing JS tooling/build systems.

**Why You? (The "Craft" Factor)**
1. You are inspired by the design-engineering cultures of companies like Linear, Vercel, Family, or Arc (The Browser Company).
2. You get annoyed when a modal closes without an exit animation.
3. You understand that latency is a UX problem, and you use optimistic UI updates to make the app feel instant.
4. You believe enterprise software doesn't have to look like a spreadsheet from 2005—it should feel as fluid as the best consumer apps.
5. You are an "AI-augmented" engineer who uses modern tools to do the work of three people while maintaining the taste of an artisan.

title: Configuration Analyst — Internal Platform & HR Operations
job_description:
**About Certa** Certa is a leading provider of AI-powered third-party risk management and workflow automation solutions. Our AI team is at the forefront of leveraging cutting-edge technologies to enhance vendor onboarding, compliance, and risk analysis.

We are looking for an Associate Product Manager (APM) to join our AI product team. This role will be instrumental in shaping AI-driven features and integrations, working at the intersection of AI, UX, and product strategy. The APM will report directly to a Senior Product Manager and collaborate closely with engineering, design, and sales teams to bring innovative AI solutions to life.

**The Role**
We are looking for a Configuration Analyst to own and evolve Certa's internal platform — a live deployment of our own product used to power HR, operations, and compliance workflows across the company. This role sits at the intersection of technical platform configuration, AI-powered process automation, and hands-on HR operations.
You will be the internal platform owner — responsible for building and maintaining workflows, ensuring the platform runs reliably, and keeping internal users well-supported. Beyond platform work, you will actively use and leverage AI tools to identify and automate HR and operational processes, and support day-to-day HR operations across the tools and platforms the HR team uses. Ad hoc operational work is a regular and expected part of this role.

**What You Will Do**

Platform Configuration & Ownership
* Partner with HR and internal stakeholders to understand needs, workflows, and pain points
* Design, configure, and maintain HR and operational processes on the Certa platform — including applicant tracking, onboarding, leave management, performance reviews, and compliance workflows
* Own the internal platform end-to-end: proactively identify issues, assess downstream impact before any change is deployed, and manage releases carefully
* Maintain clear and current documentation for all configurations, workflows, and integrations
* Ensure data confidentiality standards are upheld and report any security concerns promptly
* Integrations & Technical Work
* Integrate Certa with existing HR and operational systems (e.g., Rippling, payroll tools, ATS) for seamless data flow
* Build and maintain API-based integrations using JSON and scripting (Python / JavaScript)
* Test, troubleshoot, and resolve platform functionality issues — with a preventive mindset over a reactive one

AI-Powered Automation
* Actively use and leverage AI tools — including LLM-based assistants, automation platforms, and AI-native workflows — as a core part of how you work
* Identify HR and operational processes that can be automated or significantly improved using AI and drive implementation end-to-end
* Build AI-assisted workflows such as intelligent document generation, automated notifications, data validation, and decision-routing logic
* Stay current with emerging AI tools and continuously evaluate opportunities to reduce manual effort across HR and operations
* Bring an AI-first mindset to problem-solving — default to automation before manual execution

HR Platform Operations & Ad Hoc Support
* Support day-to-day operational tasks on HR platforms (e.g., Rippling, People Portal, payroll tools) — including employee profile updates, org changes, holiday calendar management, and workflow corrections
* Manage and execute operational requests from the HR team with urgency and accuracy — including compliance-sensitive tasks such as experience letter generation, onboarding configuration, and access management
* Handle ad hoc HR operational requests as they arise, balancing these alongside core platform and automation work
* Serve as a reliable point of contact for internal users experiencing platform-related issues
* Train and support internal staff on using the platform effectively

**You Are a Great Fit If You Have**

* A Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or Science (or equivalent hands-on experience)
* 3+ years of experience in a technical configuration, platform, or analyst role
* Hands-on experience with API (third-party) integrations and working knowledge of JSON
* Experience with scripting languages such as Python or JavaScript
* Genuine knowledge of and comfort with AI tools — you already use AI in your day-to-day work and understand how to apply it to automate real business processes
* A hands-on, problem-solving mentality — you get things done without waiting to be asked twice

title: Senior GRC & Security Governance Manager
job_description:
**About Certa**
Certa automates the vendor, supplier, and stakeholder onboarding processes for businesses
globally. Serving Fortune 500 and Fortune 1000 clients, Certa's team tackles expansive and
deeply technical challenges, driving innovation in business processes across industries. With
our rapid growth trajectory, this role offers significant opportunity for career advancement.

**About the Role**
The Senior GRC & Security Governance Manager will be responsible for owning and
maturing Certa’s enterprise-wide security governance, risk, and compliance programs.

**This is a hands-on, technically-oriented GRC role that covers:**
* ISMS ownership & policy management
* Security framework implementation
* Technical risk assessment (architecture, infrastructure, application)
* Regulatory & standards compliance (ISO 27001, SOC2, NIST, GDPR)
* Third-party and vendor security risk management
* Exception management and governance
* Security awareness and training programs
* M&A security due-diligence and integration
* The role works closely with Cloud Security, IT, Product, DevOps, Legal, and executive
* leadership to ensure security and compliance are embedded across the organization.

**What You Will Do**
**1. ISMS & Policy Management**
Own and maintain the Information Security Management System (ISMS) aligned to ISO
27001, SOC2, and relevant regulations.
Develop, maintain, and enforce security policies, standards, procedures, and baselines.
Conduct regular reviews, updates, and stakeholder alignment for all security policies.
Ensure technical implementation of policy controls across cloud, applications, and IT
infrastructure.

**2. Security Framework Implementation**
Implement and mature enterprise-wide security frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST CSF, CIS
Controls).
Map controls to technical and operational environments, including cloud, applications,
and network infrastructure.
Monitor adherence and drive continuous improvement of the security framework.

**3. Risk Management**
Lead enterprise risk assessments across architecture, cloud infrastructure, and
applications.
Identify and document risks in technical systems, configurations, and deployments.
Collaborate with Cloud Security, IT, and Product teams to remediate risks and track
closure.
Maintain a centralized risk register and automate risk monitoring where possible.

**4. Regulatory & Standard Compliance**
Manage SOC2, ISO 27001, GDPR, and other regulatory compliance initiatives.
Coordinate evidence collection, control validation, and audits with internal and external
auditors.
Drive compliance of technical controls across cloud (AWS/GCP), applications, and
endpoints.
Ensure continuous compliance monitoring and reporting.

**5. Third-Party / Vendor Risk Management**
Maintain a complete inventory of all third-party vendors, SaaS applications, and service
providers.
Conduct security assessments (SIG Lite, ISO/SOC review, technical posture evaluation).
Track and manage vendor risks and remediation activities.
Define ongoing monitoring and evidence requirements for vendor compliance.

**6. Security Awareness Programs**
Build, deliver, and maintain organization-wide security awareness programs.
Conduct targeted workshops, phishing simulations, and training campaigns.
Track and report engagement and effectiveness of awareness initiatives.

**7. Exception Management**
Implement and manage the exceptions/waiver governance process.
Review, validate, and risk-rate exception requests, define compensating controls.
Maintain records and report exception posture to leadership and auditors.

**8. M&A Security Governance**
Conduct technical security assessments for potential acquisition targets.
Evaluate cloud, network, infrastructure, and application security posture.
Define integration plans and risk mitigation for acquired entities.
Track security compliance and maturity for new entities post-acquisition.

**What You Will Need**

**Experience:** 7+ years in GRC, ISMS, risk, and compliance roles in technology or SaaS
organizations.

**Technical Breadth:** Strong technical understanding of cloud (AWS/GCP), network,
application, and infrastructure security.

**Hands-on GRC:** Proven experience with risk assessments, technical control validation,
and security evidence collection.

**Framework Mastery:** Deep knowledge of ISO 27001, SOC2, NIST, GDPR, and
industry-standard frameworks.

**Vendor Risk:** Strong experience in third-party risk management and vendor security
assessments.

**Collaboration:** Excellent communication skills to work with Engineering, Product, IT,
Legal, and external auditors.

**Good-to-Have**
ISO 27001 Lead Auditor / Lead Implementer, CISSP, CISM, CRISC, or equivalent
certifications.
Experience in M&A security due diligence.
Experience in automating compliance evidence and dashboards.
Familiarity with security awareness and training program delivery.

**Why You'll Love Working Here**
* **Direct impact:** Shape our people operations and talent acquisition strategies from the
* ground up
* **Growth path:** Clear opportunities for advancement as we scale
* **Strategic influence:** Work closely with leadership to build our people strategy
* **Cross-functional collaboration:** Partner with all departments to understand talent
* needs and drive employee success
* **Work-life balance:** Flexible remote work arrangements
* **Innovative culture:** Work with a talented, passionate team building cutting-edge
* solutions

Certa is committed to building a diverse team and strongly encourages applications from
candidates of all backgrounds. We're looking for exceptional talent who can help us secure our
platform and build trust with our enterprise clients.

title: Frontend Developer
job_description:
**Who are we? What do we do?** Over the past decade, technology has changed how tier 2+ india is consuming services - both online and offline. Imagine being in a tier 2+ city in India, sitting at the dining table of a home and discussing the monthly grocery and budget with the family. What comes to our mind first when we think of getting our monthly groceries? Our trusted neighborhood kirana store aka apne mohalle ke kirana wale bhaiyya, right?

ApnaMart started with a vision to offer an unparalleled shopping experience to tier 2+ India. A very modern shopping experience where they can choose quality products from varied assortments, add them to a shopping basket, check out at the front counter, pay cash/digitally and also give them the option to do the same thing online from the comfort of their homes through our mobile App! We are building a scalable, asset-light chain of neighborhood supermarts. We focus on bringing competitive pricing, varied assortment, and consistent customer experience coupled with the trust and personal connection of a neighborhood kirana.

We’ve come a long way from our first store to 200+ stores and 1 lac+ monthly customers. It has been an overwhelming journey! And now, we are considering scaling up at an even more incredible pace.

In short, at Apna Mart, we are redefining the grocery experience for tier 2+ cities in India. You can check our website https://apnamart.in/ for more details.

**What does technology do?** 
At ApnaMart, We are reimagining the retail stack building products that help to scale, control and drive retail operations from warehouse to store to the end customer. Systems like warehouse management and point of sale softwares help in driving the day to day operations, whereas intelligent pricing and inventory models provide the necessary controls essential to guarantee a 5 star experience for the end customer.

At the heart of our product lies a simple philosophy, to add value to the life of the end user and simplify the day to day working of each and every stakeholder. Various apps from warehouse, franchisee partner, delivery partners and the end customer, we focus on providing seamless user experience. And we do all this while planning for a scale capable of taking these systems Pan India.

Our Stack Backend: Python/Django, Postgres, Elastic, MongoDB, Celery, CI/CD, GoLang Frontend: NextJS/ReactJS Android: Kotlin/MVVM, Work Manager, Room, Firebase, Dagger Devops: Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD

**What you have:**
1+ years of developing Web Applications
Obsession with Speed, performance and great user experience.
Familiar with the usage of APIs.
Strong foundation in data structures & algorithms, OS, I/O, concurrency.
Worked on technologies ReactQuery, Zutstand, or similar.
Understands clean architecture.
Should have knowledge about versioning tools preferable GIT


**Good to have:**
Any startup experience.
Working with NextJS

title: Sr. Platform Engineer (Devops/SRE)
job_description:
## About the Role

We are looking for a **Senior Platform Engineer** to own and evolve our cloud infrastructure and internal platform. This role combines **DevOps, Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), and Platform Engineering**, with a strong focus on **scalability, reliability, security, and developer productivity**.

You will work closely with backend, data, and product teams to build a **highly available, self-service platform** on **Google Cloud Platform (GCP)** powering mission-critical systems at scale.

---

## Tech Stack

- **Cloud:** Google Cloud Platform (GCP)  
- **Orchestration:** Kubernetes (GKE)  
- **Backend:** Python (Django), Node.js  
- **Messaging:** Kafka  
- **Caching:** Redis  
- **Search:** Elasticsearch  
- **CI/CD:** GitHub Actions / GitLab CI / ArgoCD  
- **Infrastructure as Code:** Terraform  
- **Observability:** Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, ELK  
- **Security:** IAM, Secrets Management, Network Policies  

---

## What You’ll Do

### Platform & Infrastructure
- Design, build, and operate scalable **Kubernetes (GKE)** platforms on GCP  
- Own cluster architecture, networking, autoscaling, node pools, and upgrades  
- Build **internal developer platforms (IDP)** for self-service deployments  
- Manage infrastructure using **Terraform** and IaC best practices  

### Reliability & SRE
- Define and own **SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets**  
- Ensure high availability, fault tolerance, and disaster recovery  
- Lead incident response, postmortems, and root-cause analysis  
- Reduce operational toil through automation and self-healing systems  

### CI/CD & Developer Experience
- Build and maintain robust **CI/CD pipelines**  
- Enable safe deployment strategies (blue-green, canary, rollbacks)  
- Improve developer velocity through tooling, standards, and automation  
- Support clean environment separation (dev, staging, prod)

### Observability & Operations
- Implement metrics, logging, and tracing across services  
- Monitor Kubernetes workloads, Kafka, Redis, and Elasticsearch  
- Perform capacity planning and proactive performance tuning  

### Security & Cost Optimization
- Enforce least-privilege IAM and secure secrets management  
- Drive cloud cost optimization via rightsizing and autoscaling  
- Support audits, compliance, and security best practices  

---

## What We’re Looking For

### Must-Have
- Strong hands-on experience with **GCP** and **Kubernetes (production scale)**  
- Experience with **Terraform** or similar IaC tools  
- Solid understanding of **Linux, networking, DNS, TCP/IP**  
- Experience running **Python/Django and Node.js** workloads in production  
- Hands-on experience with **Kafka, Redis, and Elasticsearch**  
- Strong background in monitoring, alerting, and incident management  

### Nice-to-Have
- Experience building **Internal Developer Platforms (IDP)**  
- Knowledge of **service meshes** (Istio, Linkerd)  
- Experience with **multi-cluster or multi-region architectures**  
- Familiarity with GCP data services (BigQuery, Pub/Sub, Dataflow)  
- Experience in high-growth startup or scale-up environments  

---

Our client base features industry leaders such as Unilever, ITC, P&G, Godrej, SKF, Bajaj, Brakes India, and many other prominent manufacturing giants. Our team is the heartbeat of our success, and we are on a mission to attract top-tier talent to join us in shaping the future. If you are passionate about solving problems & thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment, we want you to be a part of our journey.
 
Do visit our website for more information or read more about us in this article by Forbes India

**About the role:**

We are looking for a talented and passionate Computer Vision Engineer to join our dynamic team and drive the development of next-generation AI solutions by working on some of the toughest problems in the domain of Intelligent Visual Inspection. As part of the ‘AI and Computer Vision’ team, you will be building, and deploying algorithms serving hundreds of real-time inferences per minute impacting manufacturing processes across diverse industries. You will also be part of the research and development initiatives to improve the existing solutions as well as to develop completely new approaches to new problems. You will work both independently and collaboratively with other teams to develop the best possible solutions.

**Key Responsibilities:**

1. Design, develop and deploy Computer Vision and Deep Learning algorithms for challenging scenarios in Intelligent Visual Quality Inspection.
2. Develop and optimize deep learning models using convolutional neural networks, object detection frameworks, and segmentation techniques
3. Optimize model performance for real-time inference while maintaining high accuracy standards
4. Research and prototype novel approaches to handle real world challenging scenarios like varying lighting conditions, surface reflections, and complex geometric patterns.
5. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to swiftly execute projects.
6. Take full ownership of projects and drive them to success.

**Key Qualifications:**

1. Bring 5-7 years of hands-on experience in deep learning and computer vision, backed by a track record of impactful industry projects or academic publications.
2. Proficient in Python with strong software engineering practices
3. Previous exposure to real-world datasets in an industrial setting is a big plus.
4. Demonstrate a profound understanding of deep learning and computer vision fundamentals.
5. Showcase your expertise in developing algorithms for image processing, content-based image analysis, object detection, segmentation, and beyond.
6. Solid understanding of deep learning frameworks such as Pytorch, Tensorflow, etc.
7. Hands-on experience with OpenCV for image processing, feature extraction and traditional computer vision techniques.
8. Excellent programming skills in Python
9. Familiarity with Cuda, Model Quantization and Optimization, (ONNX, TensorRT) will set you apart.
10. Willingness to pick up novel tasks and find novel solutions.

**Additional Requirements:**
* Proficiency with version control systems like Github or Bitbucket.
* Comfortable with Docker.

Ready to dive into the exciting world of Computer Vision with us? Apply now and be part of a team that's shaping the future of visual inspection technology!

**Why join us?**
Be a part of an exciting journey with a  growth stage startup, backed by Series-A funding from top-tier deep tech investors in Singapore and India, including Pi Ventures, Axilor, Chennai Angels, and other notable contributors. Every day at our office is a story of doing something new.
**Career Growth:** Opportunities for professional development and advancement within the company.
**Innovative Culture:** Join a forward-thinking organization that values creativity and innovation.

Join us in the pursuit of excellence as we leverage technology to overcome industry challenges. If you are passionate about scalable solutions and ready to make a significant impact, apply now and be a key player in our innovative team.

**Perks:**
* Flexible Working Hours
* Comprehensive Health Insurance
* Lunch on Us
* Gaming Zone with PS5

Location - SwitchOn HQ, HSR, Bangalore.
This Job Role requires your presence at the office. Remote working is NOT allowed.

title: Platform Software Engineer
job_description:
**POSITION SUMMARY**

EarnIn is seeking a Platform Engineer to help with strategic design, automation, and optimization of our cloud infrastructure. This role involves innovation in cloud operations, enabling rapid development and scaling solutions across teams and systems. This is an impactful role within the organization, designed for those who excel at critically evaluating the needs of a rapidly growing company and at anticipating and solving cloud challenges at scale. 

This position will be hybrid, based in our Bengaluru office (HSR Layout), as part of our expanding site. EarnIn offers our employees excellent benefits, including healthcare, internet/cell phone reimbursement, a learning and development stipend, and opportunities to travel to our Mountain View HQ. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location.

**WHAT YOU'LL DO**

- **AI-Agent Development:** Design, build, and iterate on AI agents that automate platform operations, from service and infra bootstrap, intelligent incident diagnosis, automated runbook execution, to self-healing infrastructure and PR-review bots. Own the agent lifecycle: prompt engineering, tool/function-call orchestration, evaluation, and production monitoring.
- **Workflow Automation with AI:** Identify and eliminate repetitive human-in-the-loop workflows across CI/CD, environment management, access provisioning, and change management.
- **Kubernetes Infrastructure:** Support our Kubernetes platform on AWS EKS, with a focus on environment hygiene and security; execute cluster-level tasks and updates with minimal support, and leverage AI for anomaly detection, capacity recommendations, and automated remediation.
- **Developer Experience:** Utilize our developer control plane (Cortex) to maintain paved paths and self-service actions, helping teams move from idea to production with minimal friction.
- **Observability & Excellence:** Strengthen operational excellence by monitoring SLOs/error budgets and utilizing Datadog for metrics, traces, and logs to improve system reliability.
- **Platform Development:** Develop platform services using industry best practices that enable operational excellence for Data, CI/CD, and Security.
- **Service Standardization:** Maintain service scaffolds and templates that encode testing and telemetry standards, ensure alignment with platform baselines, and use AI to auto-generate and validate configurations against them.

**WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR**

- Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
- **3+ years** in platform, infrastructure, or backend engineering with deep knowledge of Kubernetes (EKS) and cloud-native architectures on AWS.
- **Demonstrated experience building AI agents or agentic workflows**, not just using Copilot or ChatGPT, but designing multi-step AI systems that autonomously perform operational tasks (e.g., LLM-powered runbook agents, intelligent CI/CD bots, self-service assistants with tool-use/function-calling).
- **Track record of automating away repetitive workflows with AI**. You should be able to point to specific examples where you replaced manual processes with AI-driven automation that let a team do more with fewer resources.
- Experience in GitOps (ArgoCD) and CI (GitHub Actions) for multi-service systems.
- Strong coding skills in **Go** and/or **Python**, treating infrastructure as software.
- Excellent communication skills; able to clearly convey technical issues and advocate for both the team's and the customer's needs.
- Experience with LLM orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, or custom agent architectures) and prompt engineering for production systems.
- **Mission-Driven:** A strong advocate for the customer who takes initiative to fix issues and constantly asks, "can an AI agent do this instead of a human?"
**Nice to Have:**
- Experience with service mesh (e.g., Linkerd) and traffic management patterns.
- Hands-on contributions to developer productivity insights and observability for cost-aware engineering decisions.

title: Senior Software Engineer -E3
job_description:
Instawork is on a mission to create meaningful economic opportunities for skilled hourly professionals in communities around the globe. Our AI-powered labor marketplace helps local businesses scale, and enables global technology companies to push the frontiers of robotics and AI. Backed by world-class investors like Benchmark, Spark Capital, Craft Ventures, Greylock, Y Combinator, and others, we’re looking for exceptional talent to reimagine the way the world works.

About Instawork
Instawork is on a mission to create meaningful economic opportunities for skilled hourly professionals in communities around the globe. Our AI-powered labor marketplace helps local businesses scale, and enables global technology companies to push the frontiers of robotics and AI.

About Instawork Robot Labs (IRL)
We are operating as a fast-paced, 0-to-1 startup within Instawork, currently at the exact phase of growth that Scale AI experienced when LLMs first needed massive data and annotation. We are working across the world to collect data at a petabyte scale, helping frontline companies train and build cutting-edge robotics foundation models.
We have already hit a rapid scale of over 10,000 hours of data collected per day. Our unique focus is on capturing rich, diverse household data—not controlled factory environments. Our multi-pronged strategy spans across robot wranglers, robot training data, model benchmarking, and ultimately, building a marketplace to get "work" done, whether by humans or robotics.

The Role
We are looking for a highly versatile Senior/Staff Engineer with rock-solid fundamentals who can confidently tackle any problem across the tech stack. As a key engineering leader in our Bengaluru hub, you will own the technical execution for massive-scale physical AI training data, building out core systems like Instawork Lens (our global data collection platform) and Instawork Radiant (our dedicated QA platform).
You are a generalist at heart. You are just as comfortable designing a clean user experience in React as you are diving deep into AWS to tune heavy video transcoding pipelines. While we leverage agentic models heavily to accelerate our coding output, we rely on your deep engineering intuition, architectural expertise, and flawless fundamentals to ensure our systems are robust, scalable, and built the right way.

What You’ll Do

Full-Stack Versatility: Seamlessly transition between frontend, backend, and infrastructure. You will own features end-to-end, solving whatever technical challenge stands in the way of shipping.
Platform Ownership: Lead the technical development of Instawork Lens and Instawork Radiant, ensuring high-throughput data enrichment, labeling, and continuous quality assurance loops.
Infrastructure & Video Pipelines: Architect and scale our cloud environments. You will manage complex AWS infrastructure and ECS services for video transcoding, ensuring deep observability with precise telemetry across CPU, memory, network, and disk IOPS.
Architect for Scale: Design and optimize data pipelines capable of securely processing petabytes of global video and telemetry data without bottlenecking.
AI-Leveraged Engineering: Use agentic models as a force multiplier to ship faster, relying on your deep engineering fundamentals to guide the AI, spot bad patterns, and make the final architectural decisions.
Cross-Functional Mentorship: Act as a technical pillar for the team. Guide mid- and senior-level engineers through code reviews, pair programming, and architectural discussions to raise the bar for engineering excellence.

Who You Are

Master of Fundamentals: You have 6+ years of software engineering experience. You deeply understand distributed systems, database internals, and network constraints, and you write clean, maintainable code (coding ~80% of the time).
True Generalist: Extensive experience with full-stack development (Python, Django, React, or similar modern frameworks) and a willingness to learn whatever language or tool is needed for the job.
Cloud & Infrastructure Chops: Hands-on experience designing scalable backend systems and managing cloud infrastructure (AWS, ECS/Docker, CloudWatch), particularly for data-heavy or high-throughput applications.
Startup Mindset: You thrive in ambiguous, fast-paced, 0-1 environments. You act like an owner, practice high-velocity decision-making, and know how to ship product quickly without sacrificing quality.
Mission-Driven: You are genuinely excited about the robotics revolution and want to build the human advantage that powers it.
Our Values

Empathy, Trust & Candor: We build trust through honest and direct feedback.
Bias for Action: We practice high-velocity decision-making and course-correct quickly.
Always Be Learning: With AI transforming our workplace, we continuously compound our knowledge.
Act Like an Owner: Your ability to impact Instawork is limited only by your courage and conviction, not your job description.
Our Values 

Empathy, Trust & Candor
We put ourselves in the shoes of our colleagues and customers and don’t shy away from uncomfortable conversations, instead building trust through honest and direct feedback.

Bias for Action
We practice high-velocity decision-making, clear-eyed that we often operate with incomplete information. Growing quickly means it’s OK to be wrong, so long as we learn from our mistakes and course correct!

Always Be Learning
We’re a curious bunch, and with AI transforming our workplace we encourage everyone to learn from each other, compounding our knowledge and experience to help us change an entire industry.

Act Like an Owner
We work long, hard, and smart, building products that delight our users and drive growth. Your ability to impact Instawork is limited only by your courage and conviction, not your job description.

About Instawork

Founded in 2015, Instawork is the nation’s leading online labor marketplace for food services, hospitality, light industry, and logistics, connecting more than 7M skilled workers with local restaurants, hotels, warehouses, stadiums, and more. Our AI-powered platform serves thousands of businesses across more than 50 major markets in the United States and Canada. We're not just helping fill shifts, we're supporting local economies—and we're just getting started!

Instawork has been featured by CBS News, The Wall Street Journal, The Washington Post, and the Associated Press. Forbes included us on their Next Billion Dollar Startups list; RetailTech Breakthrough named us Workforce Hiring Solution of the Year for 2025; and Inc. 5000 recognized us as one of the country's top 10% fastest-growing companies two years in a row. But what matters most is our impact. We're solving real problems for real people, and we’re doing it at scale.

Join our team to help us build something that matters! We’re looking for superstars who want to help us shape the future of work. With hubs in San Francisco, Bangalore, and Chicago, city offices in New York, Phoenix, and Singapore, we're back to working together in-person five days a week because we believe the best ideas happen when great people collaborate face-to-face. We also value diverse perspectives and encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds.

Ready to make an impact? Learn more at www.instawork.com/about.

title: Forward Deployed Engineer
job_description:
**Forward Deployed Engineer**
Location: Bangalore
Focus: Enterprise Implementation, Deployment, & AI-Accelerated Development

**The Mission**
As a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) at Segmental, you are the bridge between our core product and our most strategic enterprise partners. You don’t just "support" customers; you build alongside them. Your goal is to ensure Segmental is deeply integrated into their infrastructure, solving complex data and deployment challenges in real-time.

At Segmental, we value speed and the rate of experimentation above all else. We are looking for an engineer who doesn't just write code but leverages the latest AI development tools to move 10x faster than traditional workflows.

**Key Responsibilities**
Enterprise Implementation: Lead the end-to-end technical deployment of Segmental within complex enterprise environments (Cloud, On-prem, or Hybrid).

**Rapid Prototyping**: Build custom features and integrations for clients using a "fail fast, iterate faster" mindset.

**Maintenance & Evolution**: Own the ongoing health of deployed systems while identifying opportunities for new feature development based on field insights.

**Strategic Engineering**: Act as a "product-minded" engineer, feeding customer requirements back to our core team to influence the global roadmap.

**Optimization**: Continuous performance tuning of the Segmental stack to ensure it scales with the world’s largest data workloads.

**Technical DNA**
**AI-Native Workflow**: You must be proficient with AI-assisted development tools (e.g., Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Replit Agent, or custom LLM workflows). We expect you to use AI to automate the "boring stuff" so you can focus on high-level architecture and problem-solving.

**Deployment Expertise**: Strong experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and major cloud providers (AWS/Azure/GCP).

**Full-Stack Capability**: Fluent in modern backend languages (Python, Go, or Node.js) and comfortable navigating front-end integrations.

**Data Savvy**: Experience with data pipelines, ETL processes, and large-scale database management.

**What Makes You a Great Fit?**
**High Velocity**: You prioritize a high rate of experimentation. You would rather ship a working prototype today and refine it tomorrow than spend a month in a "perfect" vacuum.

**Customer Empathy**: You can sit in a room with a CTO and a Lead Architect, speak their language, and translate their business pain into technical solutions.

**Radical Ownership**: You don't wait for tickets. You find bottlenecks and fix them. If an enterprise deployment is stalling, you are the one who unblocks it.

**Growth Mindset**: You stay on the bleeding edge of AI tools and frameworks, constantly looking for ways to accelerate your own output.

**Why Segmental?**
Segmental is where high-stakes enterprise engineering meets the agility of a lab. We are redefining how companies interact with their data, and you will be at the front lines of that transformation.

title: Founding Engineer (Full Time - Equity Based)
job_description:
**About Us**

We're building Foundinity, a co-founder matchmaking platform designed to help entrepreneurs find the right partner from the very start — reducing co-founder breakups and making startup journeys smoother. It's about intentional and behavioral design and smart matchmaking: helping entrepreneurial people find someone truly compatible to build with.

If you've ever said, *"I'd start something if I just had the right person"* — that's exactly the problem we're solving.

We've validated the concept and are now moving toward launching our first version.


**The Role**

We're looking for a Founding Engineer and you won't be a contractor or a cog — you'll have real ownership, and make decisions that shape the technical parts of the product.

You'll work directly with the founder to take Foundinity from concept to launch. That means building the MVP, making the early architectural calls, and setting the technical foundation for everything that comes after.

- Build and ship the product end-to-end
- Make key technical decisions — architecture, stack, infrastructure, integrations
- Iterate fast based on early user feedback
- Lay the groundwork for a future engineering team and culture


**About You**

This isn't a typical engineering role. It's a chance to join an entrepreneurial venture from the very beginning — to have a real stake in something you helped build from zero. We're looking for someone who's energised by uncertainty, not intimidated by it. If you're the type who wants to go big or go home, this is for you.

- Strong full-stack development background — you're comfortable owning both frontend and backend
- You've shipped products before, ideally in an early-stage environment
- You move fast without cutting corners on the things that matter
- Collaborative and communicative — you'll be working closely with a non-technical founder, so clarity matters
- Genuinely excited about the problem, not just the tech


**What We Offer**

- Meaningful equity, discussed openly and fairly
- Direct influence over technical direction, roadmap, and engineering culture
- A role at the intersection of startups, deep-tech and entrepreneurial networks
- The chance to build something that could meaningfully change how startups begin

Please note: This is a purely equity-based role. 

If you've read this far — skip the generic cover letter. We don't need one. What we do want to know is whether this actually excites you: the idea, the problem, the stage, the risk. If it does, tell us why in a few honest lines. And to show you paid attention, attach the word "**mango**" somewhere in your application. It's a small thing, but it tells us a lot.


📩 Apply or reach out directly: uddipta@foundinity.com
